entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: Practice techniques from previous levels in new mediums - watercolor, ink and brush, brush pen. Skills can be used to create beautiful collections of mottoes and quotes in a Commonplace Book or for display or gifts.

rows.label: Lettering: Level 3
rows.books: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Lettering: Level 3
☐ Be sure to have all necessary supplies available for each lesson.

☐ Watch the video tutorial on how to set up your Art Book (see Quick Links).

books.title: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

supplies.title: Fudenosuke Brush Pen Set of 2 (Hard & Soft Tip)
Beste Golden Taklon Round Brush Set

links.label: How to Set Up Your Art Book

blocks.text: Have the students spend a few minutes warming up by practicing large arm movements on the chalkboard and drawing a few strokes they have already learned.
Look at the new stroke or letter together. Allow the students to articulate what they notice. How will they achieve the desired look?

Students should practice with a small piece of chalk (on its side) on the chalkboard first so that erasing is easy. Then have students practice the new stroke, letter, or letter combination with pen and paper. They should practice until they have six perfect examples before moving on to the next stroke.
Have the students self-evaluate. They may circle their best examples or reflect on which parts were done well and which ones need more practice.

lessons.title: 20m Lettering: Level 3 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Watercolor Practice Sans Serif
⍞ Materials: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

➜ VIEW & PRACTICE
Watch the tutorial video. Practice writing your alphabet in upper and lowercase sans serif. Also practice numbers 0-9.
∞ Video Link: Lettering 3: Brush Drawing Sans Serif
⍞ Image Resource: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering p.10

➜ NOTE
You will be completing this with watercolor and round brush, size 6 or 8.

lessons.title: 20m Lettering: Level 3 - Lesson 25
lessons.body:
Words in Shapes
⍞ Materials: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

➜ NOTE
Create your projects based on the lesson title and concepts you have learned throughout your Lettering journey. You may complete these using the medium of your choice. The techniques and skills you have learned stay the same, regardless of the medium, with a few exceptions when using a brush compared to a pen.

➜ RECAP 
See pages 94-101, 122-169 in The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering for a refresher.

Exam Week  

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from: 
The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

terms.content:
· Show your work to an adult.

· Demonstrate one style of lettering in watercolor and explain it to an adult.

· Show your work to an adult.

· Demonstrate one style of lettering in ink & brush or brush pen and explain it to an adult.

· Show your work to an adult.

· Complete the monogram of a fellow student using your choice of medium.
